framework.name: NCSC Secure Design Principles
framework.scope: Principles for making technology systems secure by design, covering architecture, configuration, administration, and deployment considerations
framework.structure_description: 5 categories of secure design principles: establish the context, making compromise difficult, making disruption difficult, making compromise detection easier, and reducing the impact of compromise

controls.title: Determine a security context before designing a system
controls.title_nl: Determine a security context before designing a system
controls.description: Before designing a system, understand the threats it will face, the sensitivity of the data it will process, and the trust boundaries it will operate across. Security requirements should be derived from this context, not applied generically.
controls.description_nl: Before designing a system, understand the threats it will face, the sensitivity of the data it will process, and the trust boundaries it will operate across. Security requirements should be derived from this context, not applied generically.
controls.implementation_guidance: Conduct threat modelling during design. Identify data sensitivity levels and applicable regulations. Define trust boundaries between system components.
controls.verification_guidance: Review threat model documentation. Verify security requirements trace to identified threats.

controls.title: Determine proportionate security measures
controls.description: Security measures should be proportionate to the risk. Over-engineering security increases cost and complexity without corresponding benefit. Under-engineering leaves gaps. Calibrate controls to the threat and impact analysis.
controls.description_nl: Security measures should be proportionate to the risk. Over-engineering security increases cost and complexity without corresponding benefit. Under-engineering leaves gaps. Calibrate controls to the threat and impact analysis.
controls.implementation_guidance: Match security controls to the risk level identified in threat modelling. Document the rationale for control selection. Review proportionality when the threat landscape changes.
controls.verification_guidance: Review control selection rationale. Verify controls are proportionate to documented risks.

controls.title: Minimise the attack surface
controls.description: Reduce the number of entry points available to attackers. Remove unnecessary features, services, and code. Every additional component is a potential vulnerability. Simpler systems are easier to secure.
controls.description_nl: Reduce the number of entry points available to attackers. Remove unnecessary features, services, and code. Every additional component is a potential vulnerability. Simpler systems are easier to secure.
controls.implementation_guidance: Remove unused services and features. Minimise exposed interfaces. Reduce dependencies. Apply the principle of least functionality.
controls.verification_guidance: Review exposed interfaces and services. Verify only required functionality is deployed.

controls.title: Apply defence in depth
controls.description: Do not rely on a single security control. Use multiple layers of defence so that if one layer is compromised, others continue to protect the system. Layers should use different technologies and approaches.
controls.description_nl: Do not rely on a single security control. Use multiple layers of defence so that if one layer is compromised, others continue to protect the system. Layers should use different technologies and approaches.
controls.implementation_guidance: Implement controls at network, application, and data layers. Use different products and approaches at each layer. Verify that compromising one layer does not grant full access.
controls.verification_guidance: Map security controls by layer. Verify independence between layers. Test that single-layer compromise does not grant full access.

controls.title: Apply the principle of least privilege
controls.title_nl: Apply the principle of least privilege
controls.description: Grant users, services, and processes only the minimum permissions they need to perform their function. This limits the damage that can result from accidental misuse or account compromise.
controls.description_nl: Grant users, services, and processes only the minimum permissions they need to perform their function. This limits the damage that can result from accidental misuse or account compromise.
controls.implementation_guidance: Define minimum required permissions for each role and service account. Review access rights regularly. Use just-in-time privilege elevation for administrative tasks.
controls.verification_guidance: Audit user and service account permissions. Verify least privilege is applied. Check for excessive permissions.

controls.title: Secure by default
controls.description: Systems should be secure in their default configuration. Users should not need to take additional steps to secure the system after deployment. Default accounts, passwords, and configurations should prioritise security.
controls.description_nl: Systems should be secure in their default configuration. Users should not need to take additional steps to secure the system after deployment. Default accounts, passwords, and configurations should prioritise security.
controls.implementation_guidance: Ship systems with secure defaults. Disable unnecessary features by default. Require explicit action to reduce security. Document any security-relevant configuration options.
controls.verification_guidance: Test default configurations for security. Verify unnecessary services are off by default.

controls.title: Design for resilience
controls.description: Design systems to continue operating under adverse conditions, including during cyber attack. This means building in redundancy, failover capability, and the ability to degrade gracefully rather than fail completely.
controls.description_nl: Design systems to continue operating under adverse conditions, including during cyber attack. This means building in redundancy, failover capability, and the ability to degrade gracefully rather than fail completely.
controls.implementation_guidance: Identify single points of failure and add redundancy. Design graceful degradation paths. Implement rate limiting and circuit breakers. Test resilience through chaos engineering.
controls.verification_guidance: Review architecture for single points of failure. Test failover mechanisms. Conduct resilience exercises.

controls.title: Design for scalability under attack
controls.description: Systems should be able to absorb or mitigate volumetric attacks (DDoS) without losing availability. Design the capacity to scale resources or absorb traffic spikes, and deploy mitigation measures at the network edge.
controls.description_nl: Systems should be able to absorb or mitigate volumetric attacks (DDoS) without losing availability. Design the capacity to scale resources or absorb traffic spikes, and deploy mitigation measures at the network edge.
controls.implementation_guidance: Deploy DDoS mitigation (CDN, WAF, cloud-based scrubbing). Design for horizontal scaling. Implement rate limiting at application layer.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify DDoS mitigation is in place. Test scaling under load. Review rate limiting configuration.

controls.title: Design for easy operation and management
controls.title_nl: Design for easy operation and management
controls.description: Systems that are difficult to operate securely will inevitably be operated insecurely. Design systems with clear administrative interfaces, good logging, and straightforward configuration management.
controls.description_nl: Systems that are difficult to operate securely will inevitably be operated insecurely. Design systems with clear administrative interfaces, good logging, and straightforward configuration management.
controls.implementation_guidance: Provide clear administrative interfaces. Generate comprehensive, structured logs. Make security-relevant configuration visible and auditable.
controls.verification_guidance: Review administrative interface usability. Verify logging covers security-relevant events. Check configuration auditability.

controls.title: Enable comprehensive audit logging
controls.description: Generate audit logs for all security-relevant events. Logs should record who did what, when, from where, and the outcome. Logs should be tamper-resistant and forwarded to a central monitoring system.
controls.description_nl: Generate audit logs for all security-relevant events. Logs should record who did what, when, from where, and the outcome. Logs should be tamper-resistant and forwarded to a central monitoring system.
controls.implementation_guidance: Log authentication events, access decisions, configuration changes, and administrative actions. Use structured log formats. Forward logs to SIEM. Protect log integrity.
controls.verification_guidance: Review audit log coverage. Verify log forwarding to SIEM. Test log integrity protections.

controls.title: Limit blast radius of compromise
controls.description: Design systems so that the compromise of one component does not lead to the compromise of the entire system. Use segmentation, sandboxing, and isolation to contain breaches.
controls.description_nl: Design systems so that the compromise of one component does not lead to the compromise of the entire system. Use segmentation, sandboxing, and isolation to contain breaches.
controls.implementation_guidance: Segment networks and applications. Use container isolation. Apply micro-segmentation where feasible. Limit lateral movement through access controls.
controls.verification_guidance: Review segmentation architecture. Test lateral movement paths. Verify isolation between components.

controls.title: Design for recoverability
controls.description: Design systems to be recoverable after compromise. This means maintaining trusted backups, having rebuild capability, and being able to restore services quickly from a known-good state.
controls.description_nl: Design systems to be recoverable after compromise. This means maintaining trusted backups, having rebuild capability, and being able to restore services quickly from a known-good state.
controls.implementation_guidance: Maintain immutable backups. Automate infrastructure rebuilds (infrastructure as code). Test recovery procedures regularly. Define and test recovery time objectives.
controls.verification_guidance: Verify backup integrity and immutability. Test rebuild procedures. Measure recovery time against targets.
